skill.name: social-media-finder-skill
skill.description: Find public social media profiles for a person, brand, or business by running a BrowserAct workflow through its API.
skill.summary: Searches public social platforms for profile URLs, usernames, follower counts, and bio snippets.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for network access and environment variable access are true positives: the Python script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends authorized requests to api.browseract.com. The markdown backtick, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and critical dangerous-combination alerts are mostly false positives or expected invocation guidance; no prompt injection, obfuscation, or confirmed malicious intent was found. Publication should include a warning that people-search queries and API credentials are sent to BrowserAct.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Receives Credentialed Requests
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment, places it in an Authorization header, and sends requests to api.browseract.com. This is expected for the BrowserAct workflow, but it means credentials and task metadata leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly builds an Authorization header from the environment API key and uses it in multiple requests. The behavior is legitimate for this API wrapper, but the credential transmission risk is clear.

medium_findings.title: People-Search Queries Sent to External Workflow
medium_findings.description: The script accepts a person or brand name from the command line and sends it as the People_Name input parameter to the BrowserAct workflow. This can expose sensitive research targets and enables profile discovery use cases that require user consent and policy controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parameter flow from command-line input to the external workflow is explicit. The privacy risk depends on user behavior, so medium severity is more appropriate than a block.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Invocation
low_findings.description: The static external-command findings in SKILL.md are markdown examples that instruct the agent to run the bundled Python script. The command is not dynamically constructed in the skill file, but running local scripts still requires user trust.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a fenced bash invocation, not Ruby backtick execution or shell interpolation. It is a real execution instruction but does not show command injection.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to be substring matches in normal social media documentation and output-field text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic operations in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found ordinary prose and field names, not cryptographic code. Confidence is high because the Python script imports no crypto library.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: The skill contains agent usage guidance, API key setup instructions, and retry handling. No evidence found of override commands, claims of pre-approval, or instructions to skip security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed instructions are operational guidance for the skill. They are not framed as system messages and do not attempt to change the evaluator methodology.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Credential Used in HTTP Authorization Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it as a bearer token for outbound BrowserAct API calls. This pattern is sensitive because compromised code or logs could expose the key.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact environment variable and header construction are visible in the script. The risk is contextual but the pattern is confirmed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Network Calls to Fixed API Host
dangerous_patterns.description: The script performs POST and GET requests to a hardcoded BrowserAct workflow API host. This is core functionality, but it requires trusting the external service with submitted search targets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All outbound request sites point to the same fixed API base URL. No hidden destination was found, but third-party data transfer is confirmed.

content.value_statement: Researching public social profiles across platforms is slow and easy to miss. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow that searches a name or brand and returns profile details in a CSV.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python script that starts a BrowserAct social media finder workflow.
Searches for public profiles by a person, brand, or business name.
Polls BrowserAct task status until the workflow finishes, fails, or times out.
Returns profile details such as platform, follower count, page title, description, and URL.
Handles missing or invalid BrowserAct API keys with user-facing error messages.
Provides retry guidance for transient workflow failures.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY and access to the BrowserAct service.
Sends the submitted name or brand to BrowserAct for processing.
Only finds profiles available to the BrowserAct workflow and public web sources.
Results may include false matches that need human verification.

use_cases.title: Sales Prospect Research
use_cases.description: Find public social profiles for a prospect before outreach and verify the profiles before saving contact notes.

use_cases.title: Brand Presence Audit
use_cases.description: Check which social platforms have public accounts for a company, product, or competitor brand.

use_cases.title: Candidate Public Profile Review
use_cases.description: Locate public professional and social profiles for a candidate when the review is permitted and job relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Find One Person
prompt_templates.prompt: Find public social media profiles for Jane Smith. Use the BrowserAct social media finder and summarize likely matches.

prompt_templates.title: Research a Brand
prompt_templates.prompt: Find public social media profiles for Acme Analytics. Return the platforms, profile URLs, and any follower counts found.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Prospect Matches
prompt_templates.prompt: Find social media profiles for Jordan Lee at Northstar Labs. Flag uncertain matches and do not treat them as verified identities.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Public Presence
prompt_templates.prompt: Find public social profiles for three competitor brands, compare platform coverage, and list gaps that need manual review.

output: Summary of likely official accounts across major platforms.
Profile URLs with platform names and follower counts when available.
Notes about matches that require manual confirmation.

output_examples.input: Research social profiles for a sales prospect named Morgan Patel.
output: A concise list of possible public profiles grouped by platform.
A warning that common names can produce false matches.
Suggested next checks before using the results in outreach.

output_examples.input: Audit our brand presence for ExampleCo.
output: Found platform coverage and profile links.
Missing or uncertain platforms to review manually.
A CSV result reference when BrowserAct returns a downloadable file.

best_practices: Use this skill only for permitted research on public profiles.
Verify each match manually before making identity, hiring, or sales decisions.
Keep BrowserAct API keys in environment variables and rotate them when access changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use results to harass, stalk, or profile people without a legitimate purpose.
Do not submit sensitive personal data that is not required for the search.
Do not assume every returned profile belongs to the target person or organization.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY so the Python script can call the BrowserAct workflow API.

faq.question: What data does it send to BrowserAct?
faq.answer: It sends the provided person, brand, or business name and uses the BrowserAct API key for authorization.

faq.question: Can it find private social media accounts?
faq.answer: No. It is intended to find public profiles available through the BrowserAct workflow and public web sources.

faq.question: Are the results guaranteed to be correct?
faq.answer: No. Similar names and duplicate brands can create false matches, so users should verify important results.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with a local Python script as the invocation method.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with warnings. Users should understand the third-party API, API key handling, and privacy limits.
